      obtain ⟨ιY, _, pY, hpY, eY, _, hY⟩ := Module.IsTorsion.isPseudoIsomorphic_pi hNT
      refine hX.trans (hY.symm.trans_of_iso ?_)
      -- It suffices to show X ≅ Y where X, Y are the direct sums.
      -- They are determined by their localizations at height 1 primes.
      -- Since M_p ≅ N_p and M ~ X, N ~ Y, we have X_p ≅ Y_p.
      -- X = ⨁ A/p^e. The localizations X_p determine the multiset of exponents for p.

      let X := (i : ιX) → A ⧸ (pX i).1 ^ (eX i)
      let Y := (j : ιY) → A ⧸ (pY j).1 ^ (eY j)
      -- We construct the isomorphism X ≅ Y by combining local isomorphisms.

      have : ∀ p : PrimeSpectrum A, p.1.primeHeight = 1 →

             rw [LocalizedModule.subsingleton_iff_eq_bot, LocalizedModule.subsingleton_iff_eq_bot] at hf
             exact ⟨(LocalizedModule.map p.1.primeCompl f).toLinearEquiv (by simp [hf.1 p (le_of_eq hp)]) (by simp [hf.2 p (le_of_eq hp)])⟩
          | refl => exact ⟨LinearEquiv.refl _ _⟩
          | symm _ _ _ h => exact ⟨h.some.symm⟩

             rw [LocalizedModule.subsingleton_iff_eq_bot, LocalizedModule.subsingleton_iff_eq_bot] at hf

          | refl => exact ⟨LinearEquiv.refl _ _⟩
          | symm _ _ _ h => exact ⟨h.some.symm⟩
          | trans _ _ _ _ _ h1 h2 => exact ⟨h1.some.trans h2.some⟩
        exact ⟨e2.symm.trans (e1.trans e3)⟩
